description_long: This cowshed is a rectangular, single-story structure with a steeply pitched gable roof clad in reddish-brown metal or composite panels. The walls are constructed from horizontal wooden planks, giving it a rustic and utilitarian appearance. The roof overhangs slightly on all sides, providing some protection from the elements. The front and rear gable ends are plain, with no visible doors or windows, and the side walls are similarly unadorned. The construction suggests durability and weather resistance, suitable for housing livestock in a rural or peri-urban setting. The overall design is simple and functional, prioritizing shelter and ventilation over decorative features.
description: A rectangular wooden cowshed with a steep red gable roof and plain plank walls, designed for durability and shelter.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full expanse of the red gable roof with visible overhangs.
description_view_1: Gable end view highlighting the triangular wooden wall and the steep pitch of the roof.
description_view_2: Opposite top-down view of the roof, showing the uniformity of the red roofing material and the horizontal plank wall below.
description_view_3: Rear gable end view, similar to view 1, with plain wooden planks and no visible openings.
